items.title: Government, experts repeatedly warned of issue that caused Telstra outage

children.content: Government agencies and academics both recently warned Telstra it was vulnerable to the type of error that caused this week's national outage.

articles.title: Why Victorian trains came to a halt during the Telstra outage

children.content: Rail services for regional Victorians have resumed after a Telstra outage caused every line to be shut down for a day and a half. Why was the scale of disruption so severe?

items.title: With the economy in the doldrums, Albanese looks to AI for the answer

items.title: The meteorite at the centre of a 'culture war' between the UK and Australia

children.content: The fight to claim a 3.5-tonne space rock didn't just kickstart a publicity battle that divided an empire — it also left a gaping hole in culture. Centuries on, the question remains: Who owned it?

items.title: Girls married off as a climate change 'coping strategy'

children.content: Climate change is now believed to be a leading contributor to more frequent and younger nuptials, with 12 million girls tipped to become child brides this year.

items.title: How China's military goals in the Pacific are rapidly progressing

children.content: China's firing of a dummy warhead was the first time in two years it tested its long-range missile arsenal — and the first time ever it had launched such a rocket from a nuclear-powered submarine.

items.title: The story of Sidney Leonard Morse had been lost to history — until now

items.title: Getting guernseys reverses fortunes of one of the most remote footy clubs

children.content: The Fregon Bulldogs are among the nation's most remote Australian Rules football clubs, and for several seasons were forced to play in casual clothes. But the club has secured new guernseys — and the change in uniform has led to a change in form.

image.altText: The new design of the guernsey of the Fregon Bulldogs football club.

items.title: Historical tractors arrive at outback's 800-metre long Machinery Mile

children.content: Ilfracombe's roadside museum is almost as long as the town itself and enshrines machines from Australia's past. Locals are preparing to display the latest donation of four historical tractors.
